revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[arz:فوداراكوسان جى]][[cs:Fudarakusandži]][[en:Fudarakusan-ji]][[fa:فوداراکوسان-جی]][[fr:Fudarakusan-ji]][[it:Fudarakusan-ji]][[ja:補陀洛山寺]][[ko:후다라쿠산지]][[tr:Fudarakusan-ji]][[zh:補陀洛山寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Fudarakusanji03s2010.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tendai|qid=Q855964|1=Tendai}}|prefecture={{ill|Nachikatsuura|qid=Q1346730|1=Nachikatsuura}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.644725|N|135.93443611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q193849|1=Avalokiteśvara}}|part_of={{ill|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|qid=Q611345|1=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range}}}} {{Short description|Buddhist temple in Wakayama Prefecture, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=July 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Fudarakusan-ji
補陀洛山寺 | native_name = | image = Fudarakusanji04s2010.jpg | image_size = 300 | alt = | caption = | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 348 Hamanomiya, [[:en:Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura-chō]], [[Higashimuro District, Wakayama|Higashimuro-gun]], {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}} | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Tendai|en|Tendai|qid=Q855964|lt=Tendai|1=Tendai}} | deity = Senju [[Kannon]] (Avalokiteśvara) | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = | year_completed = 4th century (legendary) }} {{nihongo|'''Fudarakusan-ji'''|補陀洛山寺|Fudarakusan-ji}} is {{ill|Tendai|en|Tendai|qid=Q855964|lt=Tendai|1=Tendai}} temple of the [[Higashimuro District, Wakayama|Higashimuro district, Wakayama prefecture]], Japan. The name of temple comes from {{ill|Mount Potalaka|en|Mount Potalaka|qid=Q11626848|lt=Mount Potalaka|1=Mount Potalaka}}. It is said to have been founded by [[Ragyō Shōnin]], a monk from {{ill|India|en|India|qid=Q668|lt=India|1=India}}. In 2004, it was designated as part of a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|qid=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|qid=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}} under the name ''{{ill|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|en|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|qid=Q611345|lt=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|1=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range}}''. ==See also== *{{ill|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|en|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|qid=Q611345|lt=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|1=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range}} *[[Seiganto-ji]] ==External links== {{Commons Category|Fudarakusanji|Fudarakusan-ji}} {{coord|33|38|41.01|N|135|56|3.97|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Buddhist-temple-stub}}{{Kumano Faith}} [[en:Fudarakusan-ji]] [[arz:فوداراكوسان جى]] {{Commons category|Fudarakusanji}} [[cs:Fudarakusandži]] [[fa:فوداراکوسان-جی]] [[fr:Fudarakusan-ji]] [[it:Fudarakusan-ji]] [[ja:補陀洛山寺]] [[ko:후다라쿠산지]] [[zh:補陀洛山寺]] {{wikidata link|Q2585020}} [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Sanzan]] [[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]]
